title: Insurtech Lemonade Reports $148.8M In Revenue For Q4 2024
text: Lemonade reported Q4 2024 revenue of $148.8 million, representing a 29% year-over-year increase and beating analyst expectations of $144.9 million. The company's In-Force Premium (IFP), reaching $944 million marked a 26% rise, driven by expanded customer acquisition and market penetration. The insurtech firm demonstrated improved operational efficiency. Earnings per share came in at a loss of $0.42, substantially better than the anticipated $0.61 loss. Additionally, adjusted EBITDA loss narrowed to $23.8 million, beating estimates of $27.74 million, signaling progress toward profitability. For 2024 overall, Lemonade generated $48 million in adjusted free cash flow, marking a significant milestone in financial health improvement. Looking forward, the company projects 2025 revenue between $655-657 million, slightly below Wall Street's $666.52 million estimate. The guidance triggered a 9.4% stock decline in after-hours trading. CEO Daniel Schreiber emphasized that Agentic AI has reduced operational costs, supporting the company's efficiency narrative despite increased growth spending plans.
